assumptions.content: The graph, horizon, state alphabet, and ledger alphabet are finite.
assumptions.name: finite state space

assumptions.content: The ledger condition and null condition begin from the same toy state x_0.
assumptions.name: matched initial condition

assumptions.content: Both conditions receive the same abstract symbolic exogenous inputs, if any.
assumptions.name: matched symbolic inputs

assumptions.content: Ledger state does not create negative activation contribution under the chosen partial order.
assumptions.name: monotone ledger update

assumptions.content: For fixed toy state and symbolic inputs, increasing the ledger contribution cannot decrease the next toy state under the chosen order.
assumptions.name: monotone activation rule

assumptions.content: There exists at least one node and time at which the persistent ledger creates a strict toy-state advantage over the null process.
assumptions.name: witness condition

assumptions.content: The ledger_effect_size aggregation assigns positive weight to the witness difference.
assumptions.name: positive aggregation weight

blocked_or_deferred_items: No proof of full Viruse Fabric theory.

definitions.content: A finite graph-like object G=(V,E) with no biological interpretation, where V is finite and E is an abstract adjacency relation.
definitions.name: finite safe abstract toy graph

definitions.content: A bounded state vector x_t over V at time t, with x_t(v) in {0,1} or in [0,1] depending on the chosen normalized toy setting.
definitions.name: toy state

definitions.content: A finite-history state object L_t that records abstract symbolic prior activation or transfer information and persists across update steps.
definitions.name: persistent memory ledger

definitions.content: A matched toy process with the same initial state and exogenous symbolic inputs but with persistent ledger contribution removed or reset.
definitions.name: no-persistent-memory null control

definitions.content: A normalized aggregate difference between the measured memory-ledger trajectory and the no-persistent-memory null trajectory at the selected horizon or across selected horizons.
definitions.name: ledger_effect_size

planned_lemmas.claim: The memory-ledger process and the no-persistent-memory null process can be coupled on the same finite graph, initial state, horizon, and symbolic inputs.
planned_lemmas.name: coupled null construction
planned_lemmas.proof_strategy: Construct both trajectories recursively using the same non-ledger inputs and differing only in persistent ledger contribution.

planned_lemmas.claim: Under monotone ledger update and monotone activation rule, the memory-ledger trajectory is pointwise greater than or equal to the null trajectory under the chosen partial order.
planned_lemmas.name: pointwise monotonicity

planned_lemmas.claim: If pointwise dominance holds, then the normalized aggregate ledger_effect_size is nonnegative.
planned_lemmas.name: nonnegative aggregate effect
planned_lemmas.proof_strategy: Apply nonnegative aggregation weights to pointwise nonnegative differences.

planned_lemmas.claim: If a witness node and time has a strict ledger advantage and the aggregation gives it positive weight, then ledger_effect_size is strictly positive.
planned_lemmas.name: strict witness effect
planned_lemmas.proof_strategy: Separate one positive term from the nonnegative aggregate sum.

planned_lemmas.claim: If toy states and aggregation weights are normalized, then ledger_effect_size remains within the expected bounded interval.
planned_lemmas.name: bounded normalized signal
planned_lemmas.proof_strategy: Use bounded state range and normalized nonnegative weights.

proof_obligations: Define the exact toy-state order used for pointwise comparison.
Define whether x_t is binary or normalized continuous in [0,1].
Define the exact ledger update map L_{t+1}.
Define the exact null-control ledger reset or removal operation.
Define the exact transition map F for both ledger and null conditions.
Define the exact aggregation functional for ledger_effect_size.
State the witness condition in measurable finite-horizon terms.

theorem_candidates.name: Nonnegative ledger-effect theorem
theorem_candidates.statement: In a finite safe abstract toy graph system with identical initial state, identical symbolic exogenous inputs, monotone ledger update, and monotone activation rule with respect to ledger state, the memory-ledger condition has ledger_effect_size >= 0 relative to the no-persistent-memory null control.

theorem_candidates.name: Strict positive ledger-effect theorem under witness condition
theorem_candidates.statement: If the assumptions of VF-H2-TOY-THEOREM-001 hold and at least one finite-time witness node receives a ledger-dependent activation advantage that persists to the measured horizon, then ledger_effect_size > 0.

theorem_candidates.name: Bounded ledger-effect theorem
theorem_candidates.statement: For bounded binary or normalized toy states, ledger_effect_size remains within the bounded interval determined by the chosen aggregation functional, typically 0 <= ledger_effect_size <= 1 under normalized activation averaging.
